title: Methodology consult: cross-asset wall-clock uniqueness redundancy assumption
why: ALL 2026-07-03 configurations (dense state 688.6/647.4, regime-gated pooled audit, ungated sparse cusum 19,659 raw -> 654 effective, 30x concurrency) converge to effective-N ~650-690 < floor 799 because pooled_avg_uniqueness treats simultaneous events on DIFFERENT names as fully redundant. AFML §4 uniqueness concerns overlapping outcomes on the SAME price path -- two correlated-but-distinct names are partially independent bets (Grinold breadth), so full cross-asset redundancy may massively over-shrink and structurally caps every pooled equity result. This is an empirical falsification of the 'sparse asynchronous events scale effective-N' premise (CUSUM bursts synchronize across names on market-wide information arrival), making this THE binding constraint.
value_rationale: Every downstream pooled-equity result is bounded by this decision; getting the cross-asset uniqueness methodology wrong either over-shrinks all pooled results (false negatives) or under-shrinks them (false positives) depending on direction.
scope: quant-phd-advisor consult grounded in the LdP corpus: correlation-weighted or beta-adjusted cross-asset uniqueness? Keep within-name overlap purge but discount cross-name concurrency by correlation?
Reviewed spec change to pipeline/sample_weights.pooled_avg_uniqueness with an A/B parity guard on the H4 path
recommendation: Consult BEFORE any further pooled equity runs -- every downstream result is bounded by this decision.

history.reason: logged from 2026-07-03 pooled-audit B0006 readout verification pass
